findings.message: Adding "email" to frontend_rum_allowed_query_params causes the user's email address to be captured from the URL query string into RUM telemetry. This is PII collection not needed for attribution/RUM purposes (the surrounding allowlist is UTM attribution params only), and emails captured in query strings end up persisted in telemetry/analytics stores and access logs.
findings.suggestion: Remove the "email" entry from frontend_rum_allowed_query_params; keep the allowlist limited to non-PII attribution keys (utm_*). If a user identifier is genuinely required, use a non-PII pseudonymous ID rather than a raw email.
